evals.prompt: Write a technical specification for a password reset feature. Users should be able to request a reset link by email, click the link within 1 hour, and set a new password. The app is a Node.js API with PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive tech spec covering: functional requirements (request reset, validate token, set new password), non-functional (token expires in 1 hour, secure token generation, rate limiting), API endpoints (POST /auth/reset-password/request, POST /auth/reset-password), data model (password_reset_tokens table), security (timing-safe comparison, single use, HTTPS only).
expectations: Document includes functional requirements with acceptance criteria
Document specifies the token expiry (1 hour) and single-use constraint
Document includes API endpoint designs for request and reset
Document includes data model for reset tokens table
Document addresses security (rate limiting, secure token generation)
Document includes at least one non-functional requirement (performance or security)

evals.prompt: I need a technical spec for adding multi-tenant support to our SaaS API. Currently all data is in shared tables. We need to isolate data by tenant_id, all new API calls should scope to the authenticated tenant, and existing data needs migration.
evals.expected_output: Tech spec covering: architecture decision (tenant_id row-level security vs schema-per-tenant vs database-per-tenant), functional requirements (data isolation, admin super-access), non-functional (zero cross-tenant data leakage), API changes (tenant context from JWT), schema changes (add tenant_id to all tables), migration plan (backfill, multi-phase rollout).
expectations: Document covers architectural options for multi-tenancy with chosen approach
Document includes non-functional requirement for data isolation (no cross-tenant leakage)
Document describes how tenant context is determined (JWT claim, header, etc.)
Document includes schema changes (tenant_id column addition)
Document includes a migration plan for existing data
Security section addresses cross-tenant access prevention
